subject: Fwd: FW: test
body:
Do you have any interest in pursuing this with Reenstra, who is  looking for 
a 1.5% commission and can sell the helicopter immediately for $12.5  MM gross 
sales price (i.e., from which commissions have not yet been deducted),  but to 
another broker representing a buyer and the broker is asking 2.5% in  
addition to George's 1.5%?  
 
Darren K.  Indyke
Attorney-At-Law
457 Madison Avenue
4th Floor
New York, New  York 10022
Tel: 212-750-1176
Fax: 212-750-0381
e-mail: _dkiesq@aol.com_ (mailto:dkiesq@aol.com) 

------------------------------  
IRS Circular 230 Legend: Any advice contained herein was not intended or  
written to be used, and cannot be used, for the purpose of avoiding U.S.  
federal, state, or local tax penalties. Unless otherwise specifically indicated  
above, you should assume that any statement in this email relating to any U.S.  
federal, state, or local tax matter was written in connection with the promotion 
 or marketing by other parties of the transaction(s) or matter(s) addressed 
in  this email. Each taxpayer should seek advice based on the taxpayer's 
particular  circumstances from an independent tax advisor.  
================================================================= 
Confidentiality  Notice: This communication and any attachments contain 
information from Darren  K. Indyke, Esq. that constitute attorney work product or 
that are otherwise  confidential and/or privileged. Except for personal use by 
the intended  recipient, or as expressly authorized by the sender, any person 
who receives  this information is prohibited from disclosing, copying, 
distributing, and/or  using it. If you have received this communication in error, 
please immediately  delete it and all copies, and promptly notify the sender at 
the above telephone  number or electronic mail address. Nothing in this 
communication is intended to  operate as an electronic signature under applicable  
law.

From: "George Reenstra" <g.reenstra@yourjet.com>
Subject: FW: test
Date: Fri, 28 Sep 2007 15:12:54 GMT
To: dkiesq@aol.com

here is his offer, time constraint is he wants to cancel his other
helicopter and has a tight window or lose the deposit

________________________________

From: Richard Evans [mailto:Richard.evans@starspeed.co.uk] 
Sent: Friday, September 28, 2007 11:14 AM
To: George Reenstra
Subject: RE: test



Good morning George,

My conversations last night with the client were very positive and I am
now in a position to close the deal.

Could you let me have the following information at the earliest
opportunity:

*        Full helicopter specification

*        Interior photographs.

*        Where will we be able to take delivery of the machine in the
USA prior to us shipping it to the UK?

*        We would take delivery green and then get it painted in the UK

*        I assume it will have an FAA Certificate of Airworthiness
please confirm

*        Where was it fitted out? 

*        Does all the aircraft fitted equipment have EASA certification?
This is in case we want to put it on the British Register.

*        Are there factory courses for crew and engineers?

*        Can I have a copy of your draft sale contract?

*        Our standard commission is 2.5% which would be payable to
Starspeed.

*        I understand from last nights conversation the price is $12.5
Million please confirm.

*        Is it being sold to the existing position holder first and then
to my client, or sold from factory direct to my client?

*        What is the exact date that the machine is available to fly to
the embarkation port.

 

I will call later this afternoon and I am sure as the day wears on I
will have further questions.

Regards,

Richard Evans

CEO

PS this is a re-run of the original

 

________________________________

From: George Reenstra [mailto:g.reenstra@yourjet.com] 
Sent: 28 September 2007 14:42
To: Richard Evans
Subject: test

Good morning George,

My conversations last night with the client were very positive and I am now in a position to close the deal.

Could you let me have the following information at the earliest opportunity:

·        Full helicopter specification

·        Interior photographs.

·        Where will we be able to take delivery of the machine in the USA prior to us shipping it to the UK?

·        We would take delivery green and then get it painted in the UK

·        I assume it will have an FAA Certificate of Airworthiness please confirm

·        Where was it fitted out?

·        Does all the aircraft fitted equipment have EASA certification? This is in case we want to put it on the British Register.

·        Are there factory courses for crew and engineers?

·        Can I have a copy of your draft sale contract?

·        Our standard commission is 2.5% which would be payable to Starspeed.

·        I understand from last nights conversation the price is $12.5 Million please confirm.

·        Is it being sold to the existing position holder first and then to my client, or sold from factory direct to my client?

·        What is the exact date that the machine is available to fly to the embarkation port.

 

I will call later this afternoon and I am sure as the day wears on I will have further questions.

Regards,

Richard Evans

CEO
